Pariprashnena — Q&A Archive

A read-only archive of 1,235 questions and 14,977 answers from a Gauḍīya Vaiṣṇava forum (2007–2012).

Sastric origin of the Panca-Tattva Mantra??

Philosophy · asked by user [] · 2010-05-22 · 6 answers
sri krsna caitanya prabhu nityananda sri advaita gadadhara srivasadi gaura bhakta vrnda

can anyone tell me the sastra from which this mantra is taken?
user [154] · 2010-05-22
Jagannatha Dasa Babaji first instructed Bhaktivinoda Thakura in the chanting of the mantra sri krsna caitanya prabhu nityananda sri advaita gadadhara srivasadi gaura bhakta vrnda.
user [483] · 2010-05-22
ok, but is there any sastra from which it was taken?
user [38] · 2010-05-22
I found the earliest record in Jaiva Dharma, ch.11. CC 1.17.333 comes closest to Pancatattva mantra.
user [154] · 2010-05-22
Since when confidential mantras would be published? Only by a mercy of an acharya it becomes spread out. There is one similar however in Bhakti-ratnakara.
user [483] · 2010-05-22
Well, where does it say that the panca-tattva mantra is a confidential mantra and should not be printed?

I just want to know the gaudiya/vedic sastra from which the mantra is taken.
user [38] · 2010-05-22
ccd: If itd be confidential then it wouldnt be in our books. And nowadays one can find practically all confidential mantras online.
